name: Song Gunpowder Formula
description: Recorded black-powder formula using saltpeter, sulfur, and charcoal for Song military incendiaries, bombs, and fire arrows.
scopeNote: Anchored to the Wujing zongyao record of true gunpowder formulas in 1044 CE. This covers documented black-powder formulae and early military incendiary use, not later handguns, cannons, smokeless powder, or the full global diffusion of gunpowder weapons.

name: Watermills
description: Using water power for grinding grain (gristmills), sawing wood, and other industrial processes, becoming a key power source of the medieval economy.

dependencyEdges.note: Construction provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.
Waterwheel operation requires a rotating wheel-and-axle mechanism to transmit hydraulic power to milling machinery.
Basic Woodworking is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.
Hydraulics provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.
Some mills used iron parts for gearing and wear surfaces, but early watermills operated with substantial wood-and-stone construction, so this is enabling context rather than a hard requirement.

name: Mechanical Clocks
description: Weight-driven timekeeping devices made practical by verge-and-foliot escapements and gear trains, initially used in late-medieval European public clocks.
region: Europe / late-medieval tower clocks and verge escapements

dependencyEdges.note: Public clock towers and heavy weights needed buildings and supports, but the escapement and gear train are the central mechanism.
The verge escapement used metal pallets and toothed wheels, making metalworking a practical enabling capability.
Mechanical clockmaking used ratio and interval reasoning, but the source-checked mechanism is not primarily a mathematical discovery.
Water clocks were an earlier public timekeeping lineage; mechanical clocks replaced the flow regulator with weight-driven escapement control.
The verge-and-foliot mechanism depends on a toothed escape wheel and clock train, so gear technology is a direct mechanism prerequisite.

name: Spectacles (Eyeglasses)
